Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint With Proposed Second Amended Complaint Attached In Final Form And One Copy Showing The Changes And Declaration Of Counsel In Compliance With Court Rule 3.1324 Filed Concurrently
Off Calendar Mar-08-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
David Monroe, Esq. of Able & Monroe PC, 415.559.6829, for Plaintiff;
Angela Killian, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich LLP, 510.630.7640, for Defendant Salesian Society.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL MARTINEZ, and A GROUP OF PEOPLE, LLC's Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint With Proposed Second Amended Complaint.

Matter called. Ms. Killian represents that she is not contesting the tentative ruling and that she is seeking clarification as to the due date of the responsive pleading. Brief discussions are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court states that the CCP controls with regard to the responsive pleading. The Court then adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to file a second amended complaint is granted. The motion is timely given the recent discovery disclosures and there is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. (Hirsa v. Superior Court (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-489 ["That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (emphasis in original)]; California Casualty Gen. Insurance Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 278 ["[I]t is a rare case in which 'a court will be justified in refusing a party leave to amend his pleadings so that he may properly present his case.'"](disapproved on other grounds).) The trial is still months away and defendant is free to seek a trial continuance from department 206 if it believes it is necessary to conduct further discovery or prepare dispositive motions.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Park. Not Reported. (301/RCE)

PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL MARTINEZ, A GROUP OF PEOPLE, LLC'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $8750 FOR ALL THREE MOTIONS FILED CONCURRENTLY (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Request For Documents With Request Sanctions In Amount Of $8750 For All Three Motions Filed Concurrently
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David Monroe, Esq. 415-559-6829 Able & Monroe for Plaintiff.
Aimee Hamoy, Esq. 510-630-7646 Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MARTINEZ, A GROUP OF PEOPLE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Request For Documents With Request Sanctions In Amount Of $8750 For All Three Motions Filed Concurrently.

Granted. Plaintiffs properly move under CCP 2031.300 (Request for Production), CCP 2030.290 (Form Interrogatories), and CCP 2033.280 (Requests for Admissions). There is no dispute that Defendant's original responses contained both objections and substantive responses, but lacked verifications. Contrary to Defendant's arguments, under Appleton. v. Superior Court, 206 Cal. App. 3d 632 (1988), unsworn responses were equivalent to no responses. Defendant's references to the statutes regarding motions to compel further discovery responses thus are inapplicable.

Defendant subsequently served verifications and the Court finds that it thus has substantially complied with its obligations. As such, as to the RFAs, the Court will not order matters deemed admitted. (It is unclear whether a verification has been provided for the RFPs, but moving party assumes any oversight in this regard was a clerical error and will be corrected.)

However, to avoid waiver of objections, the statutory provisions require that Defendant must also make a showing of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Defendant has not made any mention of this standard nor effort to make this showing.

The Court orders Defendant to provide verified complete responses, without objection, to the RFP, RFA (Set One), and RFP (Set One), within 20 days (February 28, 2024).

Sanctions for failure to serve a timely response necessitated this motion are mandatory. In light of my finding that Defendant has provided substantially compliant responses, however, sanctions are limited to $950.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at khuibonhoa@duanemorris.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Set For Trial On Jul-08-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2023 Continued To Nov-22-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David Monroe, Esq. 415-559-6829 for Plaintiff.
Joshua Krasner, Esq. 510-630-7646 kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP for Defendant THE SALESIAN SOCIETY.

RULING - Mr. Monroe states the matter is not contested. Discussed. Kr. Krasner clarifies when the answer is due. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE SALESIAN SOCIETY'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant The Salesian Society of San Francisco's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

Count 1 (Breach of Contract): The FAC pleads promises by plaintiff that are not illusory - e.g., to sculpt and install artwork at Saints Peter and Paul Catholic Church and to pay $200,000 of the cost of doing so. (Id. at 4:1-3, Ex. A.) The FAC also adequately pleads that plaintiff's performance of the contract "was excused by Defendants' failures" to approve the artwork and by anticipatory breach. (Id. at 7:7-13.) Defendant argues that its "performance is excused because of Covid-19," but such an issue of disputed fact is not resolved at the pleading stage. Moreover, a "contract in writing may be modified by an oral agreement to the extent the oral agreement is carried out by the parties." (CACI 313.)

Count 2 (Breach of Promise/Promissory Estoppel): As already found, the promises pled by the FAC are not "too ambiguous to be enforceable" and could be relied upon. Moreover, the FAC adequately pleads damages. (Id. at 11:23-13:2.)

Count 3 (Conversion): The FAC adequately pleads that defendant excluded plaintiff from access to his sculptures. (Id. at 9:6-7.) The facts may be subject to dispute - indeed, plaintiff may eventually be proven wrong - but that is not for resolution at the pleading stage.

Count 4 (Injunctive Relief): Defendant cites no authority for its notion that Civil Code 987 only applies to completed works of art. Also, our California courts are reluctant to find preemption and have not done so regarding Civil Code 987.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
